raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint against Barclays for failing to properly investigate a credit card billing dispute involving two separate breaches of contract by XXXX XXXX XXXX and a violation of federal refund regulations ( 14 CFR 260.6 ). I purchased a single ticket itinerary ( Confirmation number : XXXX ) with a total cost of {$270.00} from XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX, for travel on XX/XX/XXXX. On the day of travel, the itinerary suffered two independent failures by the carrier : 1. Significant Delay : The first flight of the itinerary from XXXX XXXX to XXXX ( XXXX ) was delayed by 3.5 hours. Per DOT 14 CFR 260.6, any domestic delay of 3+ hours is a " Significant Delay '' that entitles the passenger to a full cash refund. 
2. Carrier Cancellation : Separately, the second flight of the itinerary from XXXX to XXXX ( XXXX ) was cancelled by XXXX. 
These were two distinct breaches of the carriage contract. Because of these failures, the entire trip became a " Trip in Vain '' the passenger was stranded at an intermediate point with no viable way to reach the destination. I was forced to purchase a new, emergency ticket on XXXX XXXX to complete the travel. ( However the purpose of travel had been defeated, as he did not reach his destination until 10 hours after he was originally supposed to land, causing him to miss a time sensitive event ) Despite receiving a 16-page evidence packet ( Faxed XX/XX/XXXX ) proving these failures, Barclays has closed my dispute ( Case # XXXX / XXXX ) twice. They are siding with XXXX deceptive claim that a 50 % partial refund for the first leg is acceptable. Legally, a flight that results in a stranded passenger due to a 3.5-hour delay and a subsequent cancellation has XXXX value and mandates a 100 % refund. By refusing to recognize a Significant Delay as defined by the DOT, Barclays has failed to conduct a 'reasonable investigation ' as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). I have paid the {$130.00} balance under protest to close my account and am seeking a full refund.
